items.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Monte Francis, Stefan Doerr, Director of the Centre for Wildfire Research at Swansea University, says that "we need to prepare the environment more to reduce the risk of fire but we will never eliminate it competely". France on Monday battled to contain a fire that scorched about 1,200 hectares in a forest south of Paris. Throughout the day, aircraft in the skies and firefighters on the ground were trying to douse the flames.

items.title: Nearly 1,000 hectares burnt in fire near Paris
items.description: A fire raging in the historic and much-visited Fontainebleau forest south of Paris on Monday has prompted evacuations and disrupted train and highway traffic. Authorities say the fire, which has destroyed nearly five percent of the 25,000-hectare forest, could have been started by arson. It comes as the region is endures its third heatwave of the ​summer, with tinder-dry vegetation and ‌high temperatures fuelling blazes. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: Two arrested as Fontainebleau forest near Paris ravaged by fire, prompting evacuations
items.description: France on Monday battled to contain a fire that scorched about 1,200 hectares in a forest south of Paris on Monday, as police arrested two people suspected of starting it. The fire erupted Sunday in the sprawling Fontainebleau forest, about 60 kilometres (40 miles) southeast of the capital, a former royal hunting estate that today is dotted with quiet villages. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris-Trent reports.

items.title: Trump says US will 'completely decimate' Iran if assassinated

items.title: US attacks Iran over ship being hit in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The United States attacked Iran early Sunday morning over an Iranian strike on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz that set the container ship ablaze and forced its crew to abandon it.

items.title: Senegal coach Pape Thiaw sacked after World Cup exit
items.description: Senegal's national footbal coach Pape Thiaw's functions were revoked in the night from Sunday to Monday. The decision is "in the best interests of Senegalese football", according to the Senegalese Football Federation.

items.title: Lock Petti to become latest Argentina centurion in Nations Championship Test
items.description: Guido Petti will become the sixth player to reach 100 caps for the Pumas when he starts against Wales in the Nations Championship this weekend in San Juan Leer más

items.title: Can ageless Lionel Messi keep delivering for Argentina at World Cup?
items.description: Aside from their diminutive genius, Argentina's most potent weapon is their remarkable camaraderie. The defending champions are now unbeaten in 11 World Cup games and the nation is dreaming of a fourth world title. Leer más

items.title: West Asia war LIVE: Trump says U.S. is reinstating maritime blockade on Iran
items.description: The United States used one-way attack sea drones in combat for the first time to strike the Iranian port of Bandar Abbas

items.title: Drone strikes kill 3, wound 5 in Moscow region: governor
items.description: Air defences shot down 81 drones over the region, Mr. Vorobyov said.

items.title: How Indian scientists are mapping the brain's last frontier
items.description: India's new brainstem atlas offers scientists an unprecedented map of one of the brain's least known regions.

items.title: Vegan pregnancy tests and other eco wins this week
items.description: Keeping cool in the heat with a millennia-old tradition in India, a German company making animal-free medical tests, and building from bottles in Mozambique.

items.title: Amnesty calls latest US third-country deportation to Eswatini unlawful
items.description: Eleven more migrants arrived in Mbabane on Wednesday as part of President Donald Trump's wide-ranging immigration crackdown.

items.title: The Chinese graduate accused of being Mexico's 'fentanyl king'
items.description: Cartel members and ex-colleagues describe how Zhang Zhidong allegedly linked Chinese chemicals to Mexican drug labs.

items.title: Andy Burnham in line to become British PM after securing party support
items.description: Burnham's bid cannot be challenged by fellow MPs, as he now has the support of almost the entire parliamentary party.

items.title: UK arrests 12 over extreme right-wing ‘terror’ threat to Muslim event
items.description: Arrests come against a backdrop of rising racial tensions and hate crimes across Britain.

items.title: Kenya: MSF trains health workers to be sent to Ebola-hit DR Congo
items.description: An Ebola training facility set up by Doctors Without Borders in Kenya is a chance for health workers to prepare for one of the most complex emergencies they are ever likely to face.

items.title: Thailand probes Bangkok bar fire that killed 28 people
items.description: More than 70 people were injured following the blaze on the outskirts of the Thai capital, officials said, the country’s worst such incident for almost two decades

items.title: Marianne: The allegory that defines France
items.description: 🇫🇷 As the face of #France, #Marianne is everywhere: the #Louvre, the Senate, even on the official government logo. This #BastilleDay, FRANCE 24's Gabrielle Nadler looks at how the allegory of Marianne was invented and what she represents today ▶️

items.title: Ex-navy commander convicted over 2017 ARA San Juan submarine tragedy
items.description: Submarine force commander Claudio Villamide handed three-year suspended sentence for negligence and breach of duties; Three other Navy officers acquitted. Leer más

items.title: Spain wildfire: Firefighters gain ground as Almería evacuees return home
items.description: Firefighters gained the upper hand on the deadly Almería wildfire after days of battling the flames, as the blaze that killed 12 people and burned 66 square kilometres began to ease, allowing some of the nearly 1,500 evacuees to return home.

items.title: Sudan: Life slowly resuming in the capital despite fears of another attack
items.description: Life is slowly resuming in several other towns just like the city after a military victory against the Rapid Support Forces (RSF).

items.title: Fontainebleau fire has decimated 5% of the forest, authorities say
items.description: France is battling to contain an "exceptionally large wildfire" in a forest south of Paris on July 13, with aircraft in the skies and firefighters on the ground trying to douse the flames amid suspicions the blaze was started deliberately. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris Trent is on the ground, and tells us more.

items.title: RSF chief Mohamed Hamdan Daglo sentenced to death in absentia
items.description: A court in Sudan's army-controlled city of Port Sudan on Sunday sentenced paramilitary leader Mohamed Hamdan Daglo and 15 others to death in absentia over charges of killing a regional governor and war crimes in Darfur, state media reported.

items.title: UK designates IRGC as terrorist organisation
items.description: The UK government has designated Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organisation under new powers.

items.title: France: hundreds dive into the Seine for Paris’ first river race in 80 years
items.description: Swimmers plunged into the Seine near the Eiffel Tower for the Open Swim Harmonie Mutuelle, the first major organised race on the river in nearly 80 years.

items.title: Locked exits, burning decor: inside Bangkok’s deadly pub fire
items.description: Survivors recount the horror after a deadly blaze engulfed a popular pub on a busy street in Bangkok.

items.title: At least 28 killed, 25 critically injured after fire engulfs Bangkok bar
items.description: Firefighters arrived at the scene in the Chatuchak district to find patrons running through flames to escape.

items.title: Lindsey Graham’s legacy: Israel advocate, Trump ally, Iran war supporter
items.description: Late US senator backed invasion of Iraq, war on Iran and approach of 'more for Israel' as he became a Trump confidant.

items.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
items.description: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say.

items.title: Standing out in a crowded workplace, By Yemi Adetayo
items.description: Organisations are constantly searching for people who can think ahead, solve problems, improve performance and create value. Become that person. Don’t wait for opportunities create the opportunities by becoming the person who makes work easier, teams stronger and organisations better. In today’s workplace, it is not the busiest employee who stands out; it is the […] The post Standing out in a crowded workplace, By Yemi Adetayo appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Ukraine loses key ally in Senator Lindsey Graham’s sudden death
items.description: Republican Senator Lindsey Graham’s death has left Ukraine without a key ally in President Donald Trump’s circle

items.title: South Korean court sentences former President to two years in jail in political funding case
items.description: Yoon Suk Yeol had denied the charges, saying he had not requested the polls or ‌promised anything in return

items.title: 'It's a monster': FRANCE 24 speaks to resident evacuated due to the Fontainebleau fire
items.description: A wildfire in in the Fontainebleau forest south of Paris has burnt nearly 1,000 hectares since July 12 - an area larger from Gibraltar. Hundreds of residents from nearby towns have had to be evacuated, as authorities are investigating what they think could have been an act of arson. FRANCE 24 spoke to one of the residents.

items.title: World Cup culture wars and the legend of Lionel Messi
items.description: World Cups don’t occur in a vacuum. Rather, they are an integral part of the construction of cultural subjectivity across the globe given the prevalence and capillarity of football. Argentina’s central role in global football and its obsessed population add complexity and intensity. Leer más

items.title: 'We're going to give everything' – Julián Álvarez on Argentina's World Cup title defence
items.description: Julián Álvarez says are determined to retain the World Cup. Leer más

items.title: New era for Gibraltar with removal of 118-year-old border controls with Spain
items.description: It is hoped that free movement between the UK territory and Spain will provide an economic boost.

items.title: Iran's supreme leader vows revenge for the killing of his father and predecessor
items.description: Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed that Iranians would continue to avenge the killing of his father - late Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei - and of other top officials killed during the war triggered by the U.S and Israeli strikes in Iran on February 28.

items.title: Gaza Strip: EU conference pledges €900m in donations
items.description: The conference in Brussels was attended by Palestinian representatives. The EU's top diplomat said the bloc's support depends on reforms in the Palestinian Authority.

items.title: As Milei’s aura fades, Argentina starts to look for a third way
items.description: With President Milei’s popularity at its lowest level in more than two years, talk of the 2027 election has taken over investor trips and political circles. The question being asked is whether it might be the moment for a third way. Leer más

items.title: Zimbabweans return home from South Africa amid anti-immigrant tensions
items.description: Zimbabweans fleeing xenophobic violence and threats in South Africa arrived at a reception centre in the border town of Beitbridge, Zimbabwe

items.title: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
items.description: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

items.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Jayden Adams dies aged 25
items.description: South Africa and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams has died. He was 25. Adams played for South Africa at the World Cup. His death was confirmed by South Africa’s minister of sport, arts and culture Gayton McKenzie.
